prompt: A cedar garden gate with a curved top rail between square timber posts. The single leaf has vertical pickets, two strap hinges on one post, and a thumb latch at the free stile. The leaf rotates on a vertical revolute hinge axis at the post, and the thumb latch rotates on a small local pivot.

A wrought-iron garden gate with a narrow arched leaf between masonry piers. The leaf has a perimeter frame, simple vertical bars, a ring latch at the meeting edge, and a short closer arm near the top rail. The leaf rotates on a vertical hinge axis at the pier, the ring latch rotates on its small pivot, and the closer arm rotates on its two support pivots.

A cantilever garden gate with a slatted aluminum leaf and a rear counterbalance tail. The long framed leaf rides between a guide post and a latch post on cantilever roller trucks, with a small latch handle at the front edge. The leaf translates prismatically along the fence line on the roller trucks, and the latch handle rotates on a small pivot at the leading stile.

A double-leaf picket garden gate for a courtyard opening. Two narrow framed leaves meet at the center, each hinged to an outer post, with one leaf carrying a latch and the other carrying a vertical cane bolt. The two leaves rotate on separate vertical revolute hinge axes at the posts, the latch rotates on a small pivot, and the cane bolt slides vertically in its guide loops.

A tubular steel side-yard gate with a support wheel under the free end. The welded leaf has a rectangular perimeter tube, horizontal rails, hinge sleeves at one post, and a trailing wheel in a swivel fork under the outer corner. The leaf rotates on a vertical hinge axis at the post, the support wheel spins continuously on its axle, and the wheel fork swivels on a vertical caster pivot.

A privacy garden gate with a solid lower panel and an open lattice upper panel. The framed leaf hangs from one side post on two hinges and closes against the opposite post with a compact rotary latch. The leaf rotates on a vertical revolute hinge axis at the post, and the latch rotates on its own short pivot at the free edge.

A bi-fold path gate with two narrow wooden panels between metal posts. The outer panel is hinged to one post, the inner panel is hinged to the first panel, and a simple slide bolt sits at the free edge. The outer panel rotates on a vertical revolute hinge at the post, the inner panel rotates on a second vertical hinge at the fold line, and the slide bolt moves prismatically in its guide.

A low picket garden gate with a lower surface bolt for holding it open. The rectangular leaf hangs on side hinges between two posts and carries a ring latch near the upper rail and a slim drop bolt near the lower rail. The leaf rotates on a vertical hinge axis, the ring latch rotates on its own pivot, and the drop bolt slides vertically through its guide brackets.

A farm-style pedestrian gate with welded wire mesh inside a steel tube frame. The single leaf hangs from one post on sleeve hinges and closes at a latch post with a simple lever latch. The leaf rotates on a vertical revolute hinge axis at the post, and the lever latch rotates on a small pivot at the meeting stile.

A self-closing garden gate for a narrow walkway. The framed wooden leaf is mounted to one post on two hinges and has a small top-mounted closer arm and a latch at the free edge. The main leaf rotates on a vertical hinge axis, the latch rotates on its own pivot, and the closer arm rotates on its two support joints as the gate swings.

A pair of cat-eye eyeglasses with a swept upper rim and slim temples. The rigid front frame carries two hinge blocks at the outer corners and small adjustable nose-pad arms near the bridge. The two temple arms fold on separate revolute hinge axes, and the nose pads rotate slightly on their small bracket pivots.

A pair of rectangular office glasses with spring-hinge temples. The front frame has straight brow lines, side hinge blocks, and a pair of slender temple arms with short spring segments near the hinges. Each temple rotates on its side hinge, and each spring segment rotates on its own small hinge axis behind the frame.

A pair of safety glasses with broad side shields and extendable temples. The front shield frame carries two hinged temple arms, and each temple has a telescoping rear segment for fit adjustment. Each temple folds on a revolute hinge at the frame corner, and each rear temple segment slides prismatically along the temple axis within its sleeve.

A pair of folding reading glasses with a center bridge hinge. The two half-fronts meet at a hinged bridge, and each half-front carries a short folding temple at its outer edge. The bridge folds on a central revolute hinge, and the two temple arms rotate on separate revolute hinges at the outer rims.

A pair of aviator glasses with a double bridge and slim metal temples. The thin front frame carries two adjustable nose-pad arms and slender temple arms attached by compact hinge blocks. The temples fold on revolute hinges at the outer corners, and the two nose pads rotate on small support-arm pivots near the bridge.

A pair of wraparound sport glasses with a curved single-piece front and straight temples. The front frame has an integrated nosepiece, and each temple is attached through a compact hinge block tucked behind the outer edge. The two temple arms rotate inward on separate revolute hinge axes at the frame ends.

A pair of browline glasses with a heavy upper bar and thin lower rims. The front carries two temple arms on outer hinges and two small nose-pad brackets projecting from the bridge. Each temple folds on its side hinge, and each nose pad rotates slightly on a small bracket pivot.

A pair of half-rim reading glasses with wire temples and a flip-up sun clip. The upper brow bar carries the main lenses and folding temples, while a secondary tinted lens frame sits ahead of the front on two small hinges. The temples fold on their outer revolute hinges, and the tinted front rotates upward on a transverse pair of revolute pivots above the main lenses.

A pair of thick-frame wayfarer glasses with broad straight temples. The rigid front frame has visible barrel hinges at the outer corners and no separate nose-pad hardware. The two temples fold inward on separate revolute hinge axes at the frame ends.

A pair of compact travel glasses with telescoping temples. The small front frame carries two hinged temple arms, and each temple includes a sliding rear section for packed storage. Each temple rotates on a side hinge at the frame corner, and each rear temple segment slides prismatically inside the forward temple sleeve.

An antique desk globe with a tilted meridian and a four-foot pedestal base. The sphere is carried between polar pivots inside the meridian, and the meridian rises from a short central post above the base. The globe rotates continuously about its polar axis, and the meridian tilts on a horizontal revolute axis through the side supports.

A classroom globe on a semicircular meridian with side adjustment knobs. The sphere sits between polar pivots inside the meridian, and the meridian is mounted to a pedestal yoke through two side trunnions. The globe spins on its polar axis, the meridian rotates on the horizontal trunnion axis, and the side knobs rotate on their own small axes.

A floor globe with a large sphere above a tripod stand and a circular support shelf. The sphere is mounted in a partial meridian, and the upper support rises from the stand through a rotating collar above the shelf. The globe rotates about its polar axis, and the upper support turns about a vertical revolute axis at the collar.

A celestial globe in an open two-post cradle on a turntable base. The sphere mounts between top and bottom polar pivots, and the cradle stands on a round rotating base plate. The sphere rotates continuously about its polar axis, and the cradle rotates about a vertical axis through the turntable base.

A tabletop globe with a separate equatorial date ring. The sphere is carried inside a partial support ring on polar pivots, and a thin date ring encircles the equator on its own supports. The globe rotates on its polar axis, and the date ring rotates continuously about the equatorial axis around the sphere.

A minimalist globe on a canted single-sided axle over a ring base. The sphere is carried by one polar spindle from a slender support arm, and the arm stands on a low circular pedestal. The sphere rotates continuously about its tilted polar axis, and the pedestal rotates about a vertical base axis.

A bar globe with an opening upper hemisphere over a short stand. The lower hemisphere is fixed to the globe body between polar pivots, and the upper hemisphere forms a lid on an equatorial hinge. The globe rotates on its polar revolute axis, and the upper hemisphere rotates on its own equatorial revolute hinge.

A wall-mounted globe with a projecting support arm and a partial meridian ring. The sphere sits between polar pivots inside the ring, and the ring is carried from the wall arm through a horizontal side trunnion. The globe rotates continuously about its polar axis, and the meridian ring tilts on the horizontal trunnion axis at the wall support.

A marine-style globe in a protective gimbal cradle on a pedestal. The globe sits between polar pivots in an inner ring, and the inner ring is carried inside an outer support ring on side pivots. The globe rotates on its polar axis, and the inner ring rotates on a horizontal revolute axis inside the outer cradle.

A compact executive globe with a low cylindrical base and a tilted support fork. The sphere is held between polar pivots inside a short forked meridian, and the whole support stands on a swiveling base collar. The globe rotates continuously about its polar axis, and the support fork rotates about a vertical axis at the base collar.

A passenger-car glove compartment with a bottom-hinged door and twin side stay arms. The storage bin is fixed in the dashboard opening, and the front door panel carries a push-button latch at the center. The door rotates downward on a horizontal lower hinge axis, the push button slides prismatically into the latch housing, and the two stay arms rotate on their support pivots.

A flush dashboard glove box with an upper-hinged door and a rotary latch knob. The storage tub is fixed behind the fascia, and the door panel is supported along its top edge with compact side friction stays behind it. The door rotates upward on a horizontal upper hinge axis, the latch knob rotates on a small local axis, and the side stays rotate on their support pivots.

A utility vehicle glove compartment with a left-hinged narrow door and a keyed cam latch. The fixed dash bin sits behind a rectangular opening, and the door is mounted to one side wall on exposed hinges. The door rotates on a vertical side hinge axis, the key cylinder rotates on its local axis, and the internal cam rotates with the lock near the latching edge.

A marine-style glove compartment with an upward-opening lid and a central push latch. The rigid storage box is recessed into the panel, and the lid is hinged at the top edge with two short side support arms. The lid rotates on a horizontal upper hinge axis, the push latch slides inward on its local axis, and the side support arms rotate on their pivots at the box and lid.

A curved dashboard glove box with a tray-like drop door. The inner storage tub is fixed to the fascia, and the outer door is connected near its lower corners by two short hinge arms with a pull handle on the face. The door rotates on the lower hinge-arm pivots, the hinge arms rotate on their support joints, and the pull handle rotates slightly on its own mount.

An off-road dashboard glove compartment with a rugged side-hinged access door. The boxy storage cavity is fixed behind the opening, and the door has a paddle latch near the free edge and exposed hinges at one side. The door rotates on a vertical revolute hinge axis, and the paddle latch rotates on its own short pivot.

A classic sedan glove box with a top-hinged woodgrain door and a small latch button. The shallow storage bin is fixed inside the dash, and the broad door is hinged along its upper edge with slim side check straps behind it. The door rotates upward on a horizontal upper hinge axis, the latch button moves prismatically into the door face, and the check straps rotate on their support pivots.

A narrow center-console glove compartment with a downward-opening door. The small fixed bin sits behind a vertical front panel, and the door has a central finger pull and two compact hinge brackets at the lower edge. The door rotates downward on a horizontal lower hinge axis, and the finger pull rotates slightly on its own small pivot.

An aircraft-style glove compartment with a side-hinged access panel and a quarter-turn latch. The fixed storage box is recessed into a flat panel, and the door is mounted on one side with two exposed hinges. The door rotates on a vertical side hinge axis, and the quarter-turn latch rotates on a short front-to-back axis in the panel face.

A utility truck glove compartment with a top-hinged panel and twin limiter links. The fixed storage bin is boxy and shallow, and the front door is hinged at the top edge with two short links near the lower corners. The door rotates upward on a horizontal top hinge axis, and the two limiter links rotate on their pivots between the door and the box walls.

A triple-fan gaming graphics card with a long shroud and a fold-out support brace at the bracket end. The card body is a flat PCB and heatsink block, three axial fans sit in circular openings, and the small brace is mounted to the bracket plate. The three fan rotors spin continuously about axes normal to the card face, and the support brace rotates on a small hinge at the bracket.

A workstation graphics card with a long shroud and a pair of centrifugal blowers near one end. The PCB and heatsink form a rectangular body, and the two blower wheels sit inside circular side housings along the top edge. Each blower rotor rotates continuously about its own axial revolute axis within the blower housing.

A compact mini-ITX graphics card with a single axial fan and a hinged anti-sag foot. The short card has a dense heatsink under a stubby shroud, and a small fold-down support foot is mounted near the bracket corner. The fan rotor spins continuously on its central axis, and the support foot rotates on a small hinge at the bracket edge.

A liquid-cooled graphics card with a pump block cover and a single small assist fan at the shroud end. The card body is a flat board with a long cover, and the assist fan sits in a circular pod at the rear corner. The assist fan rotates continuously on its central axis, and a small rotary selector knob on the shroud edge rotates on its own short shaft.

A dual-fan graphics card with a pivoting cable-cover flap over the power connector. The card has a long heat-sink body, two axial fans in a faceted shroud, and a small hinged flap near the top edge. The two fan rotors spin continuously about axes normal to the card face, and the cable-cover flap rotates on a short hinge along the top edge.

A short blower-style graphics card with a service hatch for the dust filter. The rectangular shroud encloses a single radial impeller and a small access door near the intake side. The impeller rotates continuously within its housing, and the service hatch rotates on a small hinge at the intake panel.

A large three-slot graphics card with three axial fans and a retractable support leg. The long PCB and heatsink carry a broad outer shroud, and a small support leg folds out from the underside near the far end. The three fans rotate continuously on their hubs, and the support leg rotates on a short hinge under the outer edge.

A compact two-fan card with a bracket-mounted locking tab cover. The card body is a short heatsink block in a simple shroud, and a small cover flap is mounted near the rear bracket over the latch area. The two fan rotors spin continuously about their central axes, and the cover flap rotates on a small hinge near the bracket.

A blower-and-axial hybrid graphics card with one radial fan and one open axial fan. The elongated shroud has a radial blower pod at one end and an exposed axial fan opening at the other. The radial blower rotor and the axial fan rotor each rotate continuously on their own supported axes.

A pass-through cooled graphics card with two fans and a folding handle on the top edge. The long shroud spans the board and heatsink, and a compact carry handle folds flat into a recess along the top spine. The two fan rotors spin continuously on their hubs, and the carry handle rotates on a pair of small side pivots along the top edge.

A gas barbecue grill with a cart base and a double-wall hood. The cook box is fixed on a wheeled cart, the hood spans the top on a rear hinge line, and a row of control knobs sits on the front fascia. The hood rotates upward on a rear revolute hinge, the cart wheels spin on their axle, and each control knob rotates on its own shaft.

A kettle charcoal grill on a tripod stand with a domed top lid. The round bowl sits over three legs, and the domed lid is attached at the rear with a top vent cap on the crown. The lid rotates on a rear hinge at the bowl rim, and the vent cap rotates on its central pivot above the dome.

A kamado-style ceramic grill on a rolling frame with a heavy domed lid. The oval ceramic body sits in a cradle cart, and the lid is carried by a rear hinge band with a top draft vent at the crown. The lid rotates upward on the rear hinge band, the top vent cap rotates on its small pivot, and the cart wheels spin on their axles.

An offset smoker grill with a main barrel chamber and a side firebox. The barrel sits on a wagon-style cart, the main chamber has a long top lid, and the firebox has a smaller side access door. The main lid rotates on a rear longitudinal hinge axis, the firebox door rotates on its side hinge, and the cart wheels spin on their axle.

A flat-top griddle with a full-width hinged cover on a wheeled stand. The cooking plate is fixed to the stand body, and the cover spans the griddle on a rear hinge line with a pair of front control knobs below. The cover rotates upward on a rear revolute axis, the stand wheels spin on their axles, and the control knobs rotate on their own shafts.

A pellet grill with a barrel cook chamber and a small hopper lid at one side. The barrel chamber is fixed on a cart frame, the main hood opens along the rear seam, and the pellet hopper has its own small top lid. The main hood rotates on a rear hinge axis, the hopper lid rotates on its own side hinge, and the cart wheels spin on their axle.

A portable tabletop grill with a clamshell lid and folding legs. The compact cook body carries a top lid on a rear hinge and two pairs of short support legs tucked underneath. The lid rotates on a rear hinge axis, and each folding leg rotates on a small pivot under the base.

A drum charcoal grill with a shallow hinged lid and two large side wheels. The cylindrical body is fixed to a low frame, and the shallow top lid is hinged along the rear edge above the grate. The lid rotates upward on a rear revolute hinge, and the two transport wheels spin on their common axle.

A built-in gas grill head with a broad hood and a small warming-rack hinge inside. The fixed firebox is recessed into a rectangular housing, the outer hood opens on a rear hinge, and the warming rack is mounted inside on two side pivots. The hood rotates on a rear hinge axis, and the warming rack rotates on its side pivots inside the chamber.

A compact balcony grill with a rounded lid and a side-mounted temperature knob. The cook body sits on a narrow stand, and the lid is supported from the back edge with a single small control knob on the side panel. The lid rotates upward on a rear hinge axis, and the side control knob rotates on its own small shaft.

An appliance hand truck with a tall frame and a folding toe plate. The tubular upright carries two large rear wheels, and the broad lower nose plate is hinged so it can fold flat against the frame. The two large wheels spin continuously on their axle, and the toe plate rotates on a transverse hinge at the frame base.

A convertible hand truck with a hinged frame for four-wheel cart mode. The upright handle section is joined to a lower load frame by a large pivot, with two main wheels at the rear and a secondary caster set under the platform. The main frame rotates on the central hinge for conversion, the rear wheels spin on their axle, and the caster wheels spin and swivel on their own supports.

A stair-climbing hand truck with tri-wheel clusters on both sides. The rigid frame carries one three-wheel bogie at each side near the base, and a narrow toe plate projects from the front edge. Each small wheel spins on its axle, and each three-wheel bogie rotates about its central hub relative to the frame.

A keg hand truck with a curved back frame and a folding retaining hook. The tall frame has two transport wheels at the rear and a small top hook that folds down over the load. The rear wheels spin on their axle, and the retaining hook rotates on a small hinge near the top of the frame.

A warehouse hand truck with twin rear wheels and a retractable caster module. The upright frame carries the main axle at the back and a small caster assembly tucked under the toe plate. The large wheels spin on their axle, each caster wheel spins on its local axle and swivels on its stem, and the caster module rotates on a hinge under the frame.

A folding luggage hand truck with telescoping handle rails. The compact frame has two inset wheels, a hinged toe plate at the bottom, and nested handle tubes that rise from the side rails. The handle tubes slide prismatically along the frame, the toe plate rotates on its hinge, and the wheels spin continuously on their axle joints.

A stockroom hand truck with a rounded frame and a loop handle. The frame carries a fixed toe plate, two large rear wheels, and a small kickstand leg under the back edge. The rear wheels spin on their axle, and the kickstand leg rotates on a small hinge beneath the frame.

A drum mover hand truck with a clamp arm near the upper frame. The tall chassis has two main wheels at the rear and a hinged clamp arm that swings over the load area. The rear wheels spin on their axle, and the clamp arm rotates on a transverse hinge near the upper frame.

A folding platform hand truck with a drop-down secondary shelf. The main upright frame rides on two transport wheels and carries a small shelf panel tucked behind the primary toe plate. The two wheels spin on their axle, and the secondary shelf rotates on a hinge near the lower frame.

A narrow office hand truck with two rear wheels and a fold-out parking stand. The slim tubular frame has a fixed toe plate and a short stand tucked beneath the lower crossbar. The rear wheels spin continuously on their axle, and the parking stand rotates on a small hinge under the frame.

A side-hinged casement window in a rectangular exterior frame. A glazed sash mounts to one jamb on two hinges and carries a small lever handle at the opposite side. The sash rotates on a vertical revolute hinge axis at the jamb, and the handle rotates on its own small pivot.

An awning window with a top-hinged glazed sash. The rigid outer frame is fixed, and the sash is attached along the head with two short side support arms and a compact bottom latch. The sash rotates on a horizontal upper hinge axis, the bottom latch rotates on its own pivot, and the support arms rotate on their end pivots.

A roof access hatch with a rectangular curb frame and a flat lid. The lid is supported from the rear edge by two side stays, and a pull handle sits near the front edge. The lid rotates on a rear horizontal hinge axis, the handle rotates on a small local pivot, and the two side stays rotate on their support joints.

A boat deck hatch with a smoked acrylic lid and friction stays. The low coaming frame is fixed to the deck, and the lid is hinged along one long edge with short stay arms on both sides. The lid rotates on a longitudinal hinge axis at the frame edge, and the two stay arms rotate on their end pivots as the hatch opens.

A circular porthole hatch with a thick ring frame and a dog latch. The round lid is hinged to one side of the ring, and a clamp handle sits at the opposite side. The lid rotates on a side-mounted revolute hinge axis, and the clamp handle rotates on its own short pivot.

A gullwing service hatch on a vehicle body side. The perimeter opening is fixed, and the rigid lid is attached along the upper edge with two rotary latches along the lower edge. The lid rotates on a longitudinal upper hinge axis, and the two latch dogs rotate on their short local pivots.

A utility cabinet hatch with a bottom-hinged access panel. The fixed frame surrounds the opening, and the panel carries a pair of short support chains replaced here by rigid stay bars at the sides. The panel rotates on a lower horizontal hinge axis, and the two stay bars rotate on their side support pivots.

A greenhouse vent window with a narrow top-hinged sash. The light rectangular sash is framed in aluminum and has a small center crank arm mounted below the opening. The sash rotates on a horizontal upper hinge axis, and the crank arm rotates on its two support pivots between the frame and sash.

A cellar hatch door with a thick insulated panel and a locking handle. The heavy rectangular lid is mounted on one side of the opening frame with two exposed hinges. The lid rotates on a side-mounted revolute hinge axis, and the locking handle rotates on its own pivot near the free edge.

A wall service hatch with a simple upward-opening panel. The shallow perimeter frame is fixed, and the panel is hinged along the top edge with a small rotary latch near the bottom center. The panel rotates on a horizontal upper hinge axis, and the latch rotates on its short local shaft.

A pantry cabinet with two tall framed doors and adjustable interior shelves. Each door is mounted to the cabinet sides on concealed hinges, and one meeting stile carries a compact rotary latch. The two doors rotate on separate vertical hinge axes at the cabinet sides, and the latch rotates on its own small pivot.

A wall-mounted medicine cabinet with a mirrored front door. The shallow rectangular case is fixed to the wall, and the mirrored door is attached to one side on two compact hinges. The door rotates on a vertical side hinge axis, and a small catch knob on the free edge rotates on its own short pivot.

A corner cabinet with a bifold front door. Two narrow door leaves are joined by a center hinge, and the outer leaf is mounted to the cabinet side wall on a second hinge line. The outer leaf rotates on a vertical hinge at the cabinet side, and the inner leaf rotates on a second vertical hinge at the fold line.

A metal locker cabinet with twin doors and a central handle latch. The boxy cabinet body is fixed, and the two doors are mounted on exposed side hinges with one handle assembly bridging the meeting edge. The doors rotate on vertical hinge axes at the cabinet sides, and the latch handle rotates on its own pivot at the center.

A glass-front display cabinet with two narrow framed doors. The rigid case carries glazed doors on side hinges, and each door has a small round pull knob near the meeting stile. The two doors rotate on vertical hinge axes at the side walls, and the pull knobs rotate on their local axes.

A utility base cabinet with one wide door and a tilt-out inner hamper panel. The outer cabinet body is fixed, the main door is hinged to one side, and the inner panel is hinged near its lower edge inside the opening. The main door rotates on a vertical side hinge axis, and the inner panel rotates on a lower horizontal hinge axis inside the cabinet.

A narrow broom cupboard with a full-height single door and a hold-open catch. The tall rectangular case is fixed, and the door is mounted on two side hinges with a small latch at the free edge. The door rotates on a vertical hinge axis at the cabinet side, and the latch rotates on its own short pivot.

A hutch cabinet with upper glazed doors and lower solid doors. The fixed case is divided into upper and lower sections, each with a pair of hinged doors mounted to the side walls. The four doors rotate on vertical hinge axes at the cabinet sides, and the lower latch knobs rotate on their small local shafts.

A workshop storage cabinet with double steel doors and internal pegboard panels. The rigid body supports the two doors on exposed side hinges, and each door carries a rotary handle latch near the meeting stile. The doors rotate on vertical hinge axes at the cabinet sides, and the two handle latches rotate on their local pivots.

A small bedside cabinet with one framed door and a flip-out inner tray. The outer case is fixed, the main door is hinged to one side, and the shallow inner tray is mounted on a lower hinge inside the opening. The main door rotates on a vertical side hinge axis, and the inner tray rotates on a horizontal lower hinge axis inside the cabinet.

A violin case with a shaped lower shell and a matching top lid. The rigid lower shell carries the instrument bed, and the lid is attached along one long rear edge with a carry handle at the front. The lid rotates on a rear longitudinal hinge axis, and the carry handle rotates on two small side pivots.

A trumpet case with a rectangular body and a top-opening lid. The lower shell is boxy and shallow, and the broad lid is mounted at the back edge with two front latches and a central handle. The lid rotates on a rear horizontal hinge axis, and the handle rotates on its two local pivots at the front face.

A clarinet case with a flat top lid and two side latches. The compact lower shell is fixed, and the lid spans the opening on a full-length rear hinge. The lid rotates on a rear hinge axis, and the two latches rotate on their small support pivots at the front edge.

A pedalboard case with a shallow tray base and a removable-style lid carried on one hinge line. The wide lower shell holds the pedalboard platform, and the lid is attached at the rear edge with a folding carry handle at the front. The lid rotates on a rear hinge axis, and the carry handle rotates on a pair of side pivots.

A saxophone case with an asymmetrical molded lower shell and a fitted top lid. The lower shell is deeper at one end, and the top lid follows the outline on a long rear hinge with a front latch assembly. The lid rotates on a rear longitudinal hinge axis, and the latch lever rotates on its own small pivot.

A flute case with a slim rectangular shell and a broad top lid. The rigid lower tray is shallow and narrow, and the lid is attached along the back edge with a small fold-flat handle on the front. The lid rotates on a rear hinge axis, and the fold-flat handle rotates on its two support pivots.

A compact drum-machine instrument case with a lid that opens beyond vertical. The lower shell is a simple box with side walls, and the lid is mounted on two rear hinges with a front center latch. The lid rotates on a rear hinge axis, and the center latch rotates on its small local pivot.

A guitar effects carry case with an angled lower tray and a hinged dust cover. The lower shell is broad and shallow, and the cover is attached at the rear edge with a handle and two front latches. The cover rotates on the rear hinge axis, and the handle rotates on its side pivots at the front face.

A brass-instrument case with a domed lid and a side carry handle. The shaped lower shell is fixed, and the upper lid is hinged along one long side rather than the rear edge. The lid rotates on a side-mounted longitudinal hinge axis, and the side handle rotates on two small pivots on the shell wall.

A portable synth case with a hard lower shell and a broad hinged lid. The rectangular lower base supports the instrument bed, and the lid spans the full opening on a rear hinge with a pair of front catches. The lid rotates on a rear horizontal hinge axis, and the two catches rotate on their own support pivots at the front edge.

A desktop Wi-Fi router with four external antennas and a row of front buttons. The low rectangular body carries two antennas at the rear corners and two at the side edges, with small push buttons along the front lip. Each antenna rotates on a small hinge at its base, and each front button moves on a short prismatic guide into the housing.

A gaming router with a faceted body and six folding antennas. The broad low housing has four rear antennas and one on each side, plus a rotary mode knob near the front corner. The six antennas rotate on their base hinges, and the mode knob rotates on its own short shaft in the corner pod.

A compact travel router with a fold-out two-prong power plug and one hinged antenna. The small body is boxy, with the plug tucked into one side wall and a single short antenna on the opposite edge. The plug blades rotate outward on their hinge pins, and the antenna rotates on its small base hinge.

A wall-mount router with a front service cover over the ports. The slim rectangular body has a hinged lower cover panel and two short antennas projecting from the top edge. The service cover rotates downward on a lower hinge axis, and the two antennas rotate on small base hinges along the top edge.

A rack-style router with a pair of folding handles and a front power switch. The shallow rack body has one hinged handle at each front corner and a small rocker switch near the center. The two handles rotate on their side pivots at the rack ears, and the rocker switch rotates on a short transverse pivot in the front panel.

A home mesh router with a top vent panel and a folding setup flap. The compact tower body has a small rear access flap near the base and a round setup dial on the top face. The access flap rotates on a small hinge at the back, and the setup dial rotates on its own vertical axis on the top panel.

A small 4G router with a hinged kickstand and two stub antennas. The upright rectangular body carries the kickstand on the rear face and one short antenna at each top corner. The kickstand rotates on a rear hinge near the base, and the two stub antennas rotate on their small base pivots.

An office router with a front USB cover and three rear antennas. The flat rectangular housing has a small hinged port door on the front and three articulated antennas along the rear spine. The USB cover rotates on a side hinge at the front panel, and the three antennas rotate on their individual base hinges.

A compact router with two side antennas and a sliding wireless switch. The low body has one antenna on each side wall and a short slider set into the rear edge. The two antennas rotate on small side hinges, and the wireless switch translates prismatically in its short guide slot.

A premium home router with four antennas and a rotary brightness knob. The faceted body carries two antennas at the rear and one on each side, with a small knob near the front edge for status lighting. The four antennas rotate on their base hinges, and the brightness knob rotates on its short local shaft.

A turbofan engine with a large front fan and a translating thrust-reverser sleeve. The core and front fan sit on the main axis inside a long nacelle, and the outer reverser cowl is carried by guide rails around the mid-body. A school paper cutter with a side-hinged blade arm and a fold-out extension wing. The base carries the cutting arm at one side and a short support wing folded under the far edge. The blade arm rotates on its long side hinge at the base, and the extension wing rotates on a hinge under the far edge.

A satellite dish on an azimuth-elevation bracket over a short mast. The parabolic reflector is fixed to an elevation yoke, and the yoke is mounted on a rotating collar atop the mast. The dish assembly rotates on a vertical azimuth axis at the mast collar, and the reflector rotates on a horizontal elevation axis in the yoke.

A radio telescope dish on a pedestal with a rear counterweight arm. The dish reflector is carried between side supports on an elevation trunnion, and the whole yoke stands on a round azimuth base. The pedestal rotates on a vertical azimuth axis, and the dish rotates on a horizontal elevation axis through the side trunnions.

A rooftop dish with a mesh reflector and a side-mounted feed boom. The mesh reflector is fixed to a central hub, and the hub is mounted in a U-shaped elevation fork on a short mast bracket. The mount rotates about a vertical azimuth axis at the mast, and the reflector rotates about a horizontal elevation axis in the fork.

A tracking dish on a heavy base with a hinged electronics cover on the back frame. The parabolic reflector is mounted on an elevation cradle above a rotating azimuth pedestal, and a small cover panel is cut into the rear support frame. The pedestal rotates on a vertical azimuth axis, the dish rotates on a horizontal elevation axis, and the rear cover panel rotates on a small hinge.

A compact ground dish with a deep bowl reflector on a box pedestal. The reflector is mounted on a side-supported elevation bracket, and the bracket is fixed to a rotating top plate on the pedestal. The top plate rotates on a vertical azimuth axis, and the reflector rotates on a horizontal elevation axis at the side supports.

A mobile communications dish with a fold-down transport leg and an azimuth base. The reflector and feed are carried by an elevation frame above the main pedestal, and a short support leg folds from the base frame. The pedestal rotates on a vertical azimuth axis, the reflector rotates on a horizontal elevation axis, and the transport leg rotates on a hinge at the base.

A remote dish with a circular base ring and a shallow reflector. The reflector is held by a central back frame between two side cheeks, and the cheeks sit on a low rotating base ring. The base ring rotates on a vertical azimuth axis, and the reflector rotates on a horizontal elevation axis through the side cheeks.

A large dish with a rear instrument hatch and an azimuth-elevation support. The reflector is mounted to an elevation yoke above a pedestal base, and a small hatch sits on the rear instrument box behind the dish. The base rotates on a vertical azimuth axis, the dish rotates on a horizontal elevation axis, and the hatch rotates on a small side hinge.

A shipboard communications dish on a gimbal-like azimuth-elevation pedestal. The parabolic reflector is attached to an elevation cradle, and the cradle is carried over a compact turntable base. The turntable rotates on a vertical azimuth axis, and the reflector rotates on a horizontal elevation axis at the cradle pivots.

A survey dish on a triangular base with a side tilt bracket. The reflector is fixed to a back frame carried between two side arms, and the side arms stand on a rotating center mast. The mast rotates on a vertical azimuth axis, and the back frame rotates on a horizontal elevation axis between the side arms.

A simple pet door flap in a rectangular wall frame. The rigid perimeter frame is fixed to the opening, and the flexible-looking flap is modeled as a rigid panel hinged along the top edge. The flap rotates on a horizontal upper hinge axis at the top of the frame.

An insulated pet door with a double-flap arrangement. The outer frame is fixed, with one rigid flap panel at the exterior side and a second rigid flap panel at the interior side. The two flap panels rotate independently on separate horizontal hinge axes near the tops of their respective frames.

A locking cat flap with a side sliding security panel. The fixed frame surrounds the top-hinged flap panel, and a narrow lockout panel slides in guides along one side of the opening. The flap rotates on a horizontal upper hinge axis, and the security panel slides prismatically in its side guides.

A large dog door with a rigid flap and a small latch tab at the bottom. The rectangular frame is fixed in the wall, and the flap hangs from the top edge with a latch tab near the lower center. The flap rotates on a horizontal upper hinge axis, and the latch tab rotates on its own small pivot near the flap bottom.

A pet door built into a side-hinged panel door. The outer human-sized door is fixed here as the support frame, and the small pet flap is mounted near its lower section on a top hinge. The pet flap rotates on a horizontal upper hinge axis within the larger door panel.

A tunnel pet door with an inner flap and a small weather hood outside. The fixed tunnel frame spans the wall thickness, the flap is hinged at the inner top edge, and a short hood is hinged over the exterior opening. The flap rotates on a horizontal upper hinge axis inside the tunnel, and the weather hood rotates on its own small upper hinge outside.

A pet flap with a magnetic-style lower catch and a side lock slider. The fixed frame surrounds the top-hinged flap, and a short slider is mounted on one side of the frame for locking. The flap rotates on a horizontal hinge axis at the top of the opening, and the lock slider moves prismatically in its short side slot.

A compact cat flap with a round frame and a top-hinged door panel. The circular outer ring is fixed to the opening, and the inner flap is mounted across the upper arc. The flap rotates on a horizontal hinge axis near the top of the round frame.

A pet door with a rigid flap and a hinged interior trim cover. The outer frame is fixed, the flap is mounted at the top edge, and an inner trim cover swings open on a side hinge for cleaning access. The flap rotates on a horizontal upper hinge axis, and the trim cover rotates on its side hinge at the inner frame.

A low-profile pet flap with a split double flap and a vertical lock bar. Two small rigid flaps are mounted one behind the other inside the frame, and a slim bar drops through guides along one side for locking. The two flaps rotate on separate horizontal top hinge axes, and the lock bar slides vertically in its side guides.

A pickup tailgate with a standard drop-down outer panel and a top latch handle. The gate spans the rear bed opening on two lower side hinges, and a latch handle sits near the upper center of the outer skin. The tailgate rotates downward on a horizontal lower hinge axis at the bed corners, and the latch handle rotates on its own small pivot.

A split-function pickup tailgate with a folding inner work surface. The main tailgate panel is hinged along the lower bed edge, and a secondary inner panel folds out from the top half. The main tailgate rotates on the lower horizontal hinge axis, and the inner work panel rotates on its own hinge inside the tailgate.

A side-swing pickup tailgate with a spare-tire cutout and a latch lever. The gate panel is mounted to one rear bed corner on vertical hinges and closes against the opposite corner with a lever latch. The tailgate rotates on a vertical side hinge axis, and the lever latch rotates on its own short pivot near the meeting edge.

A pickup tailgate with a fold-out step panel in the upper skin. The main gate spans the bed opening on lower hinges, and a small step panel is set into the center section of the tailgate. The tailgate rotates on the horizontal lower hinge axis at the bed, and the step panel rotates on its own hinge within the gate.

A multi-function tailgate with a split center door and a lower drop panel. The lower main gate opens downward from the bed, and a narrower center panel is mounted on a separate side hinge within the main gate. The main gate rotates on the lower horizontal hinge axis, and the center door rotates on a vertical hinge inside the tailgate.

A compact pickup tailgate with twin top latch knobs and a fold-flat work stop. The outer gate is mounted at the lower edge of the bed, and a slim work-stop bar folds from the top edge of the inner panel. The tailgate rotates on the lower horizontal hinge axis, the two latch knobs rotate on their own short shafts, and the work-stop bar rotates on a small hinge at the top edge.

A commercial pickup tailgate with a side-mounted utility hatch. The main gate is hinged at the lower bed edge, and a small access door is cut into one side of the tailgate face. The main tailgate rotates on a horizontal lower hinge axis, and the utility hatch rotates on a vertical side hinge within the gate.

A heavy-duty pickup tailgate with a fold-out grab handle. The rectangular gate panel hangs from the lower bed edge, and a short grab handle is recessed into the upper inner face. The tailgate rotates on the lower horizontal hinge axis, and the grab handle rotates on two small side pivots in its recess.

A pickup tailgate with an integrated loading ramp panel. The main tailgate is hinged at the bed floor line, and a narrower ramp panel folds out from the inner surface. The tailgate rotates on the lower horizontal hinge axis at the bed opening, and the ramp panel rotates on its own hinge within the tailgate body.

A utility pickup tailgate with a simple drop panel and a center release button. The gate spans the rear opening on two lower hinges and carries a push-release button near the top center. The tailgate rotates on the horizontal lower hinge axis, and the release button moves prismatically into the latch housing.

A flatbed platform cart with a folding push handle and four swivel casters. The rectangular deck rides on a caster at each corner, and the push handle is hinged along one short edge of the platform. The handle rotates on a long hinge at the deck edge, and each caster wheel spins on its axle while the caster fork swivels on a vertical pivot.

A warehouse platform cart with a removable side gate and two fixed rear wheels. The broad deck carries a tall push handle at one end, two fixed wheels at the back edge, and two swivel casters at the front corners. The fixed wheels spin on their axle, the front caster wheels spin and swivel on their joints, and the side gate rotates on a hinge along the deck edge.

A low stock cart with a hinged deck lip and four corner casters. The rigid rectangular platform is low to the ground, and a short retaining lip folds up from one long side. The lip rotates on a hinge along the deck side, and the four caster wheels spin on their axles while their forks swivel on vertical pivots.

A service platform cart with a folding tow handle at the front. The flat deck rides on four swivel casters, and a short tow loop folds from the front edge of the platform. The tow handle rotates on a small hinge at the platform front, and each caster wheel spins and swivels on its supported joints.

A long lumber cart with a folding rear push frame. The elongated platform has four corner casters and a tall push frame mounted on hinges at the rear edge. The push frame rotates on its hinge line at the deck rear, and the caster wheels spin on their axles while the forks swivel on vertical pivots.

A compact dolly-style platform cart with two fixed rear wheels and two front casters. The small deck has a folding push handle at one end and a mixed wheel arrangement underneath. The push handle rotates on its rear hinge line, the rear wheels spin on their axle, and the front caster wheels spin and swivel on their own supports.

A sheet-goods platform cart with a hinged stop panel at one corner. The broad flat deck rides on four swivel casters, and a short stop panel folds up from one corner to retain the load. The stop panel rotates on its hinge at the deck corner, and each caster wheel spins and swivels on its supported joints.

A postal platform cart with a folding front rail and four casters. The rectangular platform carries a short front rail on a hinge and a push handle at the rear edge. The front rail rotates on its hinge line, the rear handle rotates on its own hinge, and each caster wheel spins and swivels on its corner mount.

A steel platform cart with a hinged side handle for towing. The flat deck rides on two fixed wheels and two swivel casters, and a short side handle folds down against the deck edge. The side handle rotates on a hinge at the deck side, the fixed wheels spin on their axle, and the caster wheels spin and swivel on their joints.

A compact shop cart with a fold-flat handle and a rear parking brake bar. The rigid platform has four swivel casters and a short brake bar mounted above the rear caster pair. The handle rotates on a hinge at the deck edge, the brake bar rotates on a small pivot above the rear edge, and each caster wheel spins and swivels on its corner support.

A belt-seat playground swing on an A-frame set. The rigid top beam carries two chains down to a flexible-looking rigid seat, with each chain connection modeled as a short hanger link. The seat assembly rotates on the front and rear hanger pivots at the top beam.

A toddler bucket swing on a steel playground frame. The deep seat is hung from two side support straps connected to the top beam through short hanger joints. The bucket seat swings on the hanger pivots at the top beam supports.

A tire swing suspended from a top beam by a short rigid yoke. The circular tire seat hangs below a central support block on the beam through two short rigid links. The yoke rotates on the beam pivot, and the tire seat rotates on its hanger pivots beneath the yoke.

A board-seat swing with a folding safety bar in front of the seat. The narrow seat hangs from two side hanger links under the top beam, and a short safety bar is mounted at the seat front. The seat swings on the top hanger pivots, and the safety bar rotates on small side pivots at the seat edges.

A wide bench playground swing with side suspension arms. The bench seat hangs from two rigid side arms connected to the playground crossbeam above. The bench rotates on the upper hanger pivots at the crossbeam and on the lower pivots at the seat sides.

A disc-seat swing with a small folding footrest ring. The circular seat hangs from a compact hanger below the top beam, and a narrow ring footrest folds from the underside. The seat swings on the hanger pivot at the beam, and the footrest ring rotates on a small hinge under the seat.

A strap swing with a top swivel block and a simple seat. The seat hangs from two rigid side links connected through a small rotating block at the beam. The seat swings on the hanger pivots, and the top swivel block rotates on its own vertical pivot at the beam.

A playground swing with a slatted seat and a hinged lap bar. The seat hangs from two side hanger links under the top beam, and a short lap bar is mounted at the front corners. The seat swings on the hanger pivots at the beam, and the lap bar rotates on its side hinges at the seat edges.

A compact backyard swing with a molded seat and side chain covers. The seat is hung from two hanger links under a simple crossbeam, and each side cover is hinged over the lower chain attachment. The seat swings on the upper hanger pivots, and the two side covers rotate on their small hinges near the seat sides.

A dual-seat swing set with two independent hanging seats under one beam. The long top beam supports two separate seat assemblies on their own side hanger links. Each seat assembly rotates independently on its own pair of hanger pivots beneath the top beam.

A lotion pump bottle with a cylindrical body and a broad pump head. The bottle body is fixed, with a threaded neck carrying the pump collar, plunger head, and a short dispensing spout. The pump plunger translates prismatically along the bottle neck axis, and the dispensing spout rotates on a small revolute joint at the head.

A foaming soap pump bottle with a square body and a mushroom-shaped plunger. The rigid bottle carries a pump collar at the top and a short nozzle projecting from the plunger housing. The plunger moves prismatically along the vertical neck axis, and the nozzle rotates on its small local pivot at the pump head.

A hand-sanitizer pump bottle with a tall rectangular body and a lockable nozzle. The fixed bottle body supports a pump assembly at the neck, with a narrow nozzle that can turn to a locked position. The plunger slides prismatically along the bottle axis, and the nozzle rotates on its short axis at the pump head.

A salon product pump bottle with a large round shoulder and a long dispensing spout. The bottle body is fixed, and the pump head has a vertical plunger shaft and a long spout mounted to the upper housing. The plunger translates prismatically along the neck axis, and the spout rotates on a small joint at the head.

A dish-soap pump bottle with a squat body and a top-mounted actuator. The low rigid bottle supports a pump collar at the top and a short nozzle projecting from one side of the actuator cap. The actuator cap moves prismatically downward along the neck axis, and the nozzle rotates on its own small pivot at the cap.

A laboratory wash pump bottle with a narrow cylindrical body and a locking pump collar. The bottle is fixed below a compact pump assembly, and a short locking tab sits at one side of the collar. The plunger moves prismatically along the bottle axis, and the locking tab rotates on a small hinge at the collar.

A cosmetic pump bottle with a faceted body and a short hinged cap over the nozzle. The rigid bottle carries a pump head at the neck and a small cover cap mounted over the nozzle opening. The pump plunger translates prismatically along the bottle axis, and the cap rotates on its own small hinge over the nozzle.

A refillable soap pump bottle with a wide shoulder and a rotating spout head. The bottle body is fixed, and the pump head has a plunger with a short curved spout attached near the top. The plunger slides prismatically along the bottle neck axis, and the spout rotates on a small revolute joint at the head.

A kitchen pump bottle with a tall body and a broad press plate. The fixed bottle supports a vertical pump stem and a flat actuator plate with a side nozzle. The actuator plate and stem translate prismatically along the vertical neck axis, and the side nozzle rotates on its own short joint.

A foaming dispenser bottle with a locking pump ring and a front nozzle. The rigid body carries the pump on a threaded neck, and a narrow lock ring is mounted around the plunger shaft below the head. The plunger slides prismatically along the bottle axis, and the lock ring rotates on its own axis around the shaft.

An under-cabinet range hood with a slim visor front and two rotary control knobs. The canopy body is fixed beneath a cabinet, with a fold-down grease-filter panel under the intake and two knobs along the front lip. The filter panel rotates on a rear hinge under the canopy, and the two control knobs rotate on their own short shafts at the front edge.

A wall-chimney range hood with a hinged filter door and a rocker switch cluster. The broad hood canopy is fixed to the wall, and a rectangular intake-panel door opens from the underside for filter access. The filter door rotates on a hinge at one edge of the intake opening, and the rocker switches rotate on short transverse pivots in the front control strip.

A slide-out visor range hood with a telescoping front panel. The main housing is fixed under a cabinet, and a shallow front visor slides forward from the body with a small power slider at one side. The visor translates prismatically along its guide rails, and the power slider moves prismatically in its short side slot.

A commercial-style wall hood with removable baffle filters and a row of control knobs. The canopy body is fixed, the baffle-filter frame is carried on a hinged access panel, and the control knobs sit across the front fascia. The access panel rotates on its hinge under the canopy, and the control knobs rotate on their own local shafts.

A compact apartment range hood with a flip-open light cover and two push buttons. The shallow canopy is fixed above the cooktop, and a small light-access panel sits on a hinge at the underside near the front edge. The light cover rotates on its hinge under the canopy, and the two push buttons move on short prismatic plungers in the front face.

A telescopic range hood with a pull-out intake tray and a rotary fan dial. The outer housing is fixed in the cabinet, and the intake tray slides out from the front below a simple round dial. The intake tray translates prismatically along its side rails, and the dial rotates on its own short shaft in the front panel.

An island range hood with a hinged lower filter frame and side light knobs. The suspended canopy has a large lower opening for the filters and one small control knob on each side face near the lower edge. The filter frame rotates on a hinge along one side of the intake opening, and the side knobs rotate on their short local shafts.

A chimney hood with a fold-down front service panel and a sliding speed switch. The fixed canopy body carries the service panel at the front underside and the switch on the lower front face. The service panel rotates downward on a horizontal hinge, and the speed switch translates prismatically in its guide slot.

A built-in hood insert with a pair of hinged filter cartridges and a round timer knob. The rectangular insert body is fixed inside a cabinet, with two narrow filter doors opening from the underside. The filter doors rotate on their hinge lines at the intake opening, and the timer knob rotates on its own short shaft.

A low-profile range hood with a flip-out smoke deflector and touch-style buttons. The canopy body is fixed to the wall, and a narrow deflector flap folds out from the front edge above the intake. The deflector flap rotates on a horizontal hinge at the front edge, and the control buttons move on short prismatic plungers into the front strip.

A top-freezer refrigerator with two stacked front doors. The tall cabinet has a smaller upper freezer compartment and a larger lower fresh-food compartment, each with its own side-hinged door. The upper and lower doors rotate on separate vertical hinge axes at the cabinet side.

A side-by-side refrigerator with two tall front doors and a small service flap in one door. The tall cabinet is split into left and right compartments, and a small flap is built into the larger outer door panel. The two main doors rotate on vertical hinge axes at the cabinet sides, and the service flap rotates on a small hinge in the door face.

A French-door refrigerator with two upper doors and a lower freezer door modeled as side hinged for this category. The wide cabinet has two narrow upper compartments and a larger lower compartment behind a tall front door panel. The two upper doors rotate on vertical hinge axes at the outer cabinet sides, and the lower door rotates on its own vertical hinge axis at one side.

A compact under-counter refrigerator with a single side-hinged door and a small internal freezer flap. The boxy cabinet has one main outer door and a small flap mounted near the top inside the cavity. The main door rotates on a vertical side hinge axis, and the internal freezer flap rotates on a horizontal upper hinge axis inside the cabinet.

A beverage refrigerator with a glazed front door and a side control knob. The tall narrow cabinet has one glass-front door mounted to one side and a small thermostat knob on the inner top frame. The main door rotates on a vertical side hinge axis, and the thermostat knob rotates on its own short shaft.

A retro refrigerator with rounded corners and a prominent handle latch. The tall cabinet carries one broad door on one side hinge line, and the handle latch sits near the opposite edge. The door rotates on a vertical side hinge axis, and the handle latch rotates on its own short pivot.

A laboratory refrigerator with a split upper and lower compartment door pair. The tall cabinet has two separate front doors, one above the other, each mounted on side hinges at the same cabinet side. The upper and lower doors rotate independently on their own vertical hinge axes.

A freezer-on-bottom refrigerator with two upper French doors and a small access panel in one door. The upper fresh-food section is split into two narrow doors, and one door carries a short rectangular hatch on its outer face. The two main upper doors rotate on vertical hinge axes at the outer cabinet sides, and the hatch rotates on a small side hinge in the door panel.

A shallow office refrigerator with a single side-hinged door and a reversible latch lever. The compact rectangular cabinet has one main front door and a short lever near the top edge. The door rotates on a vertical hinge axis at one cabinet side, and the latch lever rotates on its own pivot near the top corner.

A pantry refrigerator with twin doors and fold-out lower bottle rails inside one door. The tall cabinet is divided into two side-by-side compartments, and one door carries a short folding bottle-retainer rail on the inside. The two doors rotate on vertical hinge axes at the cabinet sides, and the bottle rail rotates on a small hinge on the inner door panel.

A remote weapon station with a rotating base, a tilting gun cradle, and a sensor pod. The pedestal supports a yaw ring, the yaw ring carries a gun cradle between side plates, and the sensor pod is fixed beside the weapon. The base rotates on a vertical yaw axis, and the gun cradle rotates on a horizontal pitch axis between the side plates.

A roof-mounted weapon station with a boxy shield and a hinged ammunition-cover lid. The turret sits on a rotating ring above the roof, and the gun cradle is mounted inside the shield with a separate lid over the ammunition box. The turret rotates on a vertical yaw axis, the cradle rotates on a horizontal pitch axis, and the ammunition-cover lid rotates on a small hinge on the box top.

A compact weapon station with a pan-tilt support and a side service panel. The lower pedestal supports a rotating upper body, and the weapon mount is carried on a short elevation yoke with a service panel in the side skin. The pedestal rotates on a vertical axis, the weapon mount pitches on a horizontal axis, and the side panel rotates on a small hinge.

A stabilized weapon station with a rounded sensor pod and a folding guard arm. The broad turret body sits on a yaw base, and a slim guard arm folds alongside the gun cradle. The turret rotates on a vertical yaw axis, the cradle rotates on a horizontal pitch axis, and the guard arm rotates on its own small hinge beside the cradle.

A heavy remote weapon station with a rotating base and a dual-side elevation fork. The lower ring carries a rigid fork, and the weapon housing sits between the fork arms with a small top access hatch. The base rotates on a vertical axis, the weapon housing rotates on a horizontal axis between the fork arms, and the access hatch rotates on a short top hinge.

A compact naval remote station with a rotating pedestal and a side-opening sensor cover. The turret body sits above the pedestal, with the gun mount at the front and a sensor cover mounted to one side. The pedestal rotates on a vertical yaw axis, the gun mount pitches on a horizontal axis, and the sensor cover rotates on its side hinge.

A vehicle weapon station with a low-profile turret and a hinged reload door. The turret sits on a rotating base ring, and the main gun cradle is mounted at the front with a door on the side of the feed housing. The turret rotates on a vertical axis, the gun cradle rotates on a horizontal pitch axis, and the reload door rotates on its side hinge.

A modular weapon station with a square base, a tilting launcher module, and a camera mast. The base supports the yaw stage, while the module is carried between side cheeks and the camera mast folds at the rear corner. The base rotates on a vertical yaw axis, the launcher module rotates on a horizontal pitch axis, and the camera mast rotates on its rear hinge.

A compact remote station with a pan base and a tilt frame carrying a machine gun and optics block. The round pedestal supports the tilt frame, and a small shield flap folds from the front of the optics block. The pedestal rotates on a vertical yaw axis, the tilt frame rotates on a horizontal pitch axis, and the shield flap rotates on a small hinge.

A roof-mounted remote station with a rotating turret, a tilting weapon cradle, and a top service lid. The turret shell stands on a yaw ring over the roof, and the service lid spans the top of the weapon housing. The turret rotates on a vertical axis, the weapon cradle rotates on a horizontal pitch axis, and the service lid rotates on a top hinge along one edge.

A snap-lock utility knife with a sliding blade carriage and a thumb slider on top. The rigid handle body surrounds a central blade carrier, and the slider sits in a long guide slot along the spine. The blade carriage slides prismatically along the handle axis, and the thumb slider translates with it in the top guide slot.

A heavy-duty utility knife with a rotating lock wheel and a sliding blade carrier. The broad handle body supports the internal carriage and a round lock wheel near the front side. The blade carriage slides prismatically along the handle axis, and the lock wheel rotates on its own short shaft in the side wall.

A compact box cutter with a retractable blade and a hinged blade-change door. The short handle body encloses the blade carriage, and a small service door is mounted near the nose. The blade carriage slides prismatically along the handle axis, and the service door rotates on a small side hinge near the front.

A utility knife with a fold-out spare-blade magazine in the tail. The main handle encloses the sliding blade carriage, and a small tray folds from the rear end cap. The blade carriage slides prismatically along the handle axis, and the spare-blade tray rotates on a hinge at the tail.

A slim utility knife with a top thumb slider and a side safety latch. The rigid handle shell surrounds the blade track and carries a small latch tab in the side wall. The blade carriage slides prismatically along the nose-to-tail axis, and the safety latch rotates on its own small pivot in the handle side.

A metal-body utility knife with a retractable blade and a rotating rear cap latch. The handle is a straight metal shell with the blade carriage inside and a cap latch at the tail end. The blade carriage slides prismatically along the handle axis, and the rear cap latch rotates on its own short pivot.

A utility knife with a sliding carriage and a hinged belt clip. The rigid handle body has a top slider for the blade and a fold-flat clip mounted on one side wall. The blade carriage slides prismatically along the handle axis, and the belt clip rotates on a small hinge on the handle side.

A compact safety knife with a retractable blade and a front guard flap. The handle encloses the blade carriage, and a short guard flap is hinged at the nose opening. The blade carriage slides prismatically along the handle axis, and the guard flap rotates on a small hinge at the front opening.

A broad utility knife with a side-mounted blade lock lever. The straight handle surrounds the sliding blade track and carries a short lever near the center. The blade carriage slides prismatically along the handle axis, and the blade lock lever rotates on a small side pivot.

A contractor utility knife with a sliding blade carriage and a folding lanyard ring. The robust handle body carries the thumb slider on top and a small ring folded into the tail. The blade carriage slides prismatically along the handle axis, and the lanyard ring rotates on a small hinge at the tail.

A studio ring light on a telescoping tripod stand with a tilt yoke. The circular light head sits in a U-shaped yoke at the top of the stand, and the stand has a nested center column over three folding legs. The light head rotates on a horizontal tilt axis in the yoke, the center column slides prismatically in the stand mast, and the tripod legs rotate on their hinges at the hub.

A desktop ring light on a weighted base with a phone clamp. The circular light head is mounted on a tilt bracket over a short support post, and a small phone clamp is fixed inside the ring on a side pivot. The light head rotates on a horizontal tilt axis at the bracket, and the phone clamp rotates on its small pivot inside the ring.

A ring light on a boom stand with a folding side handle. The ring head is mounted on a tilt yoke at the end of a short boom, and the stand has a telescoping main mast with folding legs. The ring head rotates on its tilt axis, the mast slides prismatically along the stand axis, and the legs rotate on their hinges at the base hub.

A cosmetic ring light with a telescoping center post and a hinged mirror panel below the ring. The circular light is fixed to a top bracket, and a small mirror panel hangs below on a short hinge. The ring support post slides prismatically for height adjustment, and the mirror panel rotates on its small lower hinge below the ring.

A floor ring light with a rolling base and a tilting light head. The circular light sits in a yoke at the top of a central mast, and the mast rises from a low caster base. The light head rotates on a horizontal tilt axis, the caster wheels spin on their axles and swivel on their stems, and the mast slides prismatically for height adjustment.

A ring light with a phone holder on a pivoting arm inside the ring. The ring head is fixed to a tilt yoke at the top of a telescoping stand, and the phone holder is mounted on a short arm across the inner opening. The ring head rotates on a tilt axis, the stand column slides prismatically, and the phone holder rotates on its own small pivot arm inside the ring.

A compact ring light with a fold-flat tabletop stand. The circular light head sits on a short tilt bracket, and the small three-leg stand folds outward from the base. The light head rotates on a horizontal tilt axis, and the three stand legs rotate on small hinges at the base hub.

A ring light on a camera stand with a rotating brightness knob and telescoping mast. The circular head sits at the top of the stand in a fixed bracket, and a round control knob is mounted on the rear of the light housing. The mast slides prismatically for height adjustment, and the control knob rotates on its own short shaft on the rear housing.

A salon ring light with a side-mounted accessory arm. The ring head is held in a tilt yoke above a telescoping stand, and a short accessory arm folds from one side of the stand collar. The ring head rotates on its horizontal tilt axis, the stand column slides prismatically, and the accessory arm rotates on a small side hinge.

A portable ring light with a telescoping stand and a folding carry handle on the head. The circular head is mounted in a tilt bracket at the top of the stand, and a slim carry handle folds flat against the outer ring. The head rotates on a horizontal tilt axis, the stand column slides prismatically, and the handle rotates on two small pivots on the ring housing.
